title: salvia leucophylla
intro: Salvia leucophylla, also known as White Sage or Purple Sage, is a perennial shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used medicinally for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and relieve congestion. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a good night's sleep. Salvia leucophylla can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
